headline: Dow Jones Movers: IBM Leads, Sherwin-Williams Drags as Analysts Clash on Salesforce
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (NYSE: DIA) had a rough week, falling 2.95% as tariff anxiety, macro uncertainty, and a VIX spike to 23.75 rattled blue-chip investors. That fear gauge is now sitting at its 88th percentile over the past year, we’ll see if that continues as much of this week’s sell-off came from worries the ... Dow Jones Movers: IBM Leads, Sherwin-Williams Drags as Analysts Clash on Salesforce

headline: Marc Benioff Said Mass AI Layoffs Weren't Coming. Then Came A Brutal Week For White-Collar Jobs.
summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff downplayed fears of AI causing mass layoffs, but Morgan Stanley, Oracle, Capital One made headlines on job cuts during the same week. A comment that didn't age well?
